description_long: This is a large, double-headed battle axe with a dark metallic finish and a robust, angular design. The axe features two broad, symmetrical blades with beveled edges and a central ridge, giving it a formidable and intimidating appearance. The head is adorned with a raised geometric emblem and shield motif, adding a decorative and possibly symbolic element. The handle is long, straight, and reinforced, with a textured grip section and a pointed pommel at the base, which may serve as a secondary striking surface. There are subtle metallic accents and a small, gold-colored band near the lower part of the handle. The overall construction suggests durability and strength, suitable for heavy-duty use or ceremonial display.
description: A large, double-bladed battle axe with a dark metallic finish, geometric emblem, and a long, reinforced handle with gold accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, symmetrical blades, central emblem, and full handle with gold accent visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and handle, with the geometric ridge and grip details visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the back of the emblem and the full blade span.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the handle's gold band and the blade's edge profile.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight handle with a rectangular cross-section, reinforced with metallic and leather grip features, and a pointed pommel.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical blades with beveled edges, central ridge, and a raised geometric emblem; no beard, with a decorative shield motif.
attribute_mounting_style: Modern reinforced mounting with integrated head and handle, likely composite or pinned.
